location: Random drop (roughly 4%) from a whip-wielding Fire Prelate. The commonly farmed one stands north of the Giants' Mountaintop Catacombs entrance, northeast of Zamor Ruins in the Mountaintops of the Giants, just before a long narrow bridge. (Whip-wielding Fire Prelates also appear in Mt. Gelmir and inside Giant-Conquering Hero's Grave, but the Mountaintops one next to the catacombs is the standard farming spot.)
tactics: This Fire Prelate variant hits far harder than most field enemies — many players find it tougher than an actual boss, so don't underestimate it or fight it carelessly in the open.
Lure it to the Giants' Mountaintop Catacombs entrance doorway; it can't follow you inside, letting you chip it down safely from the doorway with a bow or ranged sorcery/incantation.
Eat a Silver-Pickled Fowl Foot (+50 Discovery for 3 minutes) right before the killing blow to cut down on repeat farming trips for the 4% drop.
Don't confuse this whip-wielding Fire Prelate with the more common hammer-wielding variant elsewhere — only the whip-wielder can drop the Thorned Whip.
lore: A heavy, thorn-wrapped lash carried by the Prelates who lead the Fire Monks, fashioned to evoke the tangled briars of sin as a tool of religious punishment and discipline.

builds.name: Pure Dexterity bleed
builds.why: Dexterity scaling dwarfs Strength, and the innate 50 blood loss buildup makes it an efficient hemorrhage weapon straight off the drop, no infusion required.

builds.name: Blood-infused endgame bleed
builds.why: As a regular, infusable weapon it can take a Blood affinity, trading some raw Dexterity scaling for Arcane scaling and pushing blood loss buildup even higher for an Arcane/bleed build.
